channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Deep hole, 12th June
item.description:
Deep hole on grassed area to the right as you come in through the gate of the playground, before the picnic bench

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blenheim Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked storm drain, 12th June
item.description:
The drain is blocked
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cline Road, Muswell Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bulking rubbish left infront of 1 Willowdene, 12th June
item.description:
It’s summer so children are playing outside. It’s obstructing the pathway and the children are playing with the bulky rubbish. It’s a health and safety risk I asked number 1 if it’s their rubbish and they said no, it’s been there a few days and nobody has removed it

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carlton Grove, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhanging foliage obstructing footpath, 12th June
item.description:
There is an abundance of stinging nettles and brambles overhanging the footpath through Kingswood Glen. It has a high footfall as is used frequently by pupils from Highfield schools and members of the community.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, Kingswood Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large dead hedgehog on the grass, 12th June
item.description:
Dynkery road outside number 260
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dunkery Road, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loads of bottles and rubbish in grass verge, 12th June
item.description:
This is next to smarden grove dunkery road side so much rubbish along here

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dunkery Road, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large tree needs pruning on pavement side, 12th June
item.description:
Very large tree needs pruning

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lynwood Grove, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loads of rubbish been left next to electic cupboard, 12th June
item.description:
Rubbish been dumped by electrical cupboard
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tenterden Close, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large bits of wood dumped on grass, 12th June
item.description:
Large bits of wood dumped on grass near number 7 smarden grove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Prestbury Square, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repeated Illegal Obstruction of the Highway, 12th June
item.description:
Evening 12 June 2026 - Yet again the Public Highway has metal railings obstructing the section of road designated for cyclists. I presume the person(s) responsible for this obstruction do not possess a licence from Bexley for this lane closure. Even if they did, these railings do not comply with Chapter 8. They are an unnecessary hazard to vulnerable road users. Are the railings restricting the use of the footway sanctioned by Bexley Council? Should a person suffer loss or injury, who will be pursued for compensation?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Westwood Lane, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car parked on double yellows daily, 12th June
item.description:
It’s becoming really dangerous to try and drive around Aldi/junction/Kidbrooke Park Road - people are now parking on double yellow lines infront of the new flats - and now cars on both sides conceal the road ahead or a clear view. Can someone please come up with a solution - parking tickets don’t seem to work.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kidbrooke Park Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 40 king Henry’s road, London nw3 3qt, 12th June
item.description:
The builders putting wood planks to reserve parking without getting authorization from Camden council or getting parking suspension from council to suspend 3 parking bays.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, King Henry's Road, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip, 12th June
item.description:
Bags of rubbish and hedges and grass dumped on pathway blocking
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Sayes Court Road, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Domestic items abandoned, 12th June
item.description:
These have been here for a couple of days now. By number 69 and 63.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dunlace Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping at the junction of Ivymount and Glennie Roads, 12th June
item.description:
A mattress and cardboard boxes dumped overnight at the end of Ivymount and Glennie Roads.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ivymount Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple potholes, 12th June
item.description:
Just as you cycle down hill on the road close to the place where I have pinpointed the road is severely damaged and many potholes are there. Please fix because it's been like this for at least the past four years.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sternhold Avenue, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping by the tree in front of the car dealer business, 12th June
item.description:
A large white board or door panel, propped up against the tree, with chipped edges and a ragged hole punched through it — there's a small metal handle visible, so it may be a cupboard door or part of flat-pack furniture A darker, weathered board behind it A clear plastic storage tub filled with tangled green garden netting, wooden stakes, and debris Several grey bin bags piled to the right A small black plastic bag (possibly a dog waste bag) in the foreground

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Palace Road, Tulse Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken red plastic sofa fly-tipped in Ashburnham Retreat near back of Ashburnham Arms, 12th June
item.description:
Broken red plastic sofa fly-tipped in Ashburnham Retreat near back of Ashburnham Arms

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ashburnham Grove, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped rubble and wood, 12th June
item.description:
Bags of rubble and wood dumped on Lincoln Road by Agricola Place

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): James Street, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The slide on playground not safe, 12th June
item.description:
Hi, The slide structure on the playground isn’t safe. It has windows where there is nothing and so a baby or toddler can fall out from there, hit the ground, and break. Please can this be fixed so the slide can be used. Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cygnet Square, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
